tool.name: WGCNA

configfiles.configfile:
library(WGCNA) library(igraph) options(stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
## Galaxy parameters counts_file <- "$counts_file" metadata_file <- "$metadata_file" network_type <- "$network_type" tom_type <- "$tom_type" cor_type <- "$cor_type" min_module_size <- as.integer($min_module_size) merge_cut_height <- as.numeric($merge_cut_height) me_cor_threshold <- as.numeric($me_cor_threshold) plot_resolution <- as.integer($plot_resolution)
log_transform <- $log_transform filter_by_variance <- $filter_by_variance variance_quantile <- as.numeric($variance_quantile) run_sample_gene_qc <- $run_sample_gene_qc
## Use GALAXY_SLOTS if set by the cluster runner, fall back to 1 otherwise n_threads <- as.integer(Sys.getenv("GALAXY_SLOTS", unset = "1")) allowWGCNAThreads(nThreads = n_threads)
## corFnc/adjacency()/TOMsimilarity() expect the *function name* ("cor" or ## "bicor"), whereas blockwiseModules()'s corType expects "pearson"/"bicor". ## Keep both derived from the single user-facing cor_type parameter so the ## correlation method is consistent across every step of the pipeline. cor_fnc <- ifelse(cor_type == "bicor", "bicor", "cor")
## Read soft power from config file if provided, else use manual value #if $softpower_config: sft_config <- readLines("$softpower_config") soft_power_line <- grep("^soft_power\\s*=", sft_config, value = TRUE)[1] if (is.na(soft_power_line) || !nzchar(soft_power_line)) { stop("Could not find a 'soft_power' entry in the provided soft-thresholding config file.") } soft_power <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(trimws(strsplit(soft_power_line, "=", fixed = TRUE)[[1]][2]))) if (is.na(soft_power)) { stop("The soft power value read from the config file is not a valid integer.") } ## Cross-checking the network_type and variance_quantile that has been recorded in the soft-thresholding ## config. A network_type mismatch is fatal as signed vs. unsigned softpowers are not comparable, hence stopping the script. ## wheras, a variance_quantile mismatching means that the power was estimated on a slightly different filtered gene set, resulting ## it to be a warning and not a hard failure. cfg_network_type_line <- grep("^network_type\\s*=", sft_config, value = TRUE)[1] if (!is.na(cfg_network_type_line) && nzchar(cfg_network_type_line)) { cfg_network_type <- trimws(strsplit(cfg_network_type_line, "=", fixed = TRUE)[[1]][2]) if (nzchar(cfg_network_type) && cfg_network_type != network_type) { stop("Network type in soft-thresholding config (", cfg_network_type, ") does not match the selected network type (", network_type, ").") } } cfg_variance_line <- grep("^variance_quantile\\s*=", sft_config, value = TRUE)[1] if (filter_by_variance && !is.na(cfg_variance_line) && nzchar(cfg_variance_line)) { cfg_variance_quantile <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(trimws(strsplit(cfg_variance_line, "=", fixed = TRUE)[[1]][2]))) if (is.finite(cfg_variance_quantile) && !isTRUE(all.equal(cfg_variance_quantile, variance_quantile))) { warning("Variance quantile in soft-thresholding config (", cfg_variance_quantile, ") does not match the selected variance_quantile (", variance_quantile, ").") } } cat("Soft power loaded from config file:", soft_power, "\n") #else: soft_power <- as.integer("$soft_power") cat("Soft power set manually:", soft_power, "\n") #end if
## trait_columns arrives as a comma-separated string from the Galaxy ## data_column parameter (with use_header_names=true this is column names, ## e.g. "age_at_diagnosis,sample_group"; in tool tests it may be indices). trait_tokens <- trimws(strsplit("$trait_columns", ",")[[1]]) trait_tokens <- trait_tokens[nzchar(trait_tokens)] if (length(trait_tokens) == 0) { stop("No trait columns selected.") }
cat("Config loaded. Starting WGCNA analysis...\n")
## Build a numeric trait matrix from a metadata data.frame. ## Numeric columns are used as-is; non-numeric (categorical) columns are ## one-hot encoded via model.matrix(). NA values in categorical columns are ## kept as their own explicit "NA" level rather than being silently dropped ## (model.matrix() drops NA rows by default, which would otherwise cause ## samples with missing categorical traits to disappear from the matrix). prepare_trait_matrix <- function(meta, sample_col = 1) { sample_ids <- meta[[sample_col]] meta_data <- meta[, -sample_col, drop = FALSE]
## Collect one piece (a numeric vector or a one-hot matrix) per column, ## then combine them all in a single do.call(cbind, ...) rather than ## repeatedly growing the result with cbind() inside the loop. pieces <- list() for (col_name in names(meta_data)) { col_values <- meta_data[[col_name]]
if (is.numeric(col_values)) { pieces[[col_name]] <- col_values } else { col_factor <- factor(col_values, exclude = NULL) levels(col_factor)[is.na(levels(col_factor))] <- "NA" mm <- model.matrix(~ col_factor - 1) colnames(mm) <- paste0(col_name, "_", levels(col_factor)) pieces[[col_name]] <- mm } }
result <- as.data.frame(do.call(cbind, pieces)) rownames(result) <- as.character(sample_ids) return(result) }
## ── STEP 1: Load data ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── counts <- read.delim(counts_file, check.names = FALSE, row.names = 1) ## Note: row.names = 1 is intentionally NOT used here, since the first ## column (sample IDs) is also needed further down as a regular data column ## (e.g. for the duplicate-sample check and when building the trait matrix). meta <- read.delim(metadata_file, check.names = FALSE)
## Sanity check: the samples in the counts matrix (columns) must match the ## samples listed in the first column of the metadata file. if (!setequal(colnames(counts), meta[[colnames(meta)[1]]])) { stop("Sample names in the counts matrix columns do not match the sample ", "names in the first column of the metadata file.") }
cat("Data loaded:", nrow(counts), "genes x", ncol(counts), "samples\n")
## ── STEP 2: Prepare expression matrix ─────────────────────────────────────── expr_data <- as.matrix(counts) mode(expr_data) <- "numeric"
if (log_transform) { expr_data <- log2(expr_data + 1) }
if (any(is.na(expr_data))) { expr_data <- expr_data[complete.cases(expr_data), , drop = FALSE] cat("Removed rows with missing values. New dimensions:", dim(expr_data), "\n") }
gene_variances <- apply(expr_data, 1, var, na.rm = TRUE) gene_variances[!is.finite(gene_variances)] <- NA_real_
filtered_expr_data <- expr_data variance_threshold <- NA_real_
if (filter_by_variance) { valid_var <- gene_variances[is.finite(gene_variances)] if (length(valid_var) == 0) { warning("No finite variances found; skipping variance filtering.") } else { variance_threshold <- quantile(valid_var, variance_quantile, na.rm = TRUE) filtered_expr_data <- expr_data[gene_variances > variance_threshold, , drop = FALSE] if (nrow(filtered_expr_data) == 0) { warning("Variance filter removed all genes; using unfiltered expression matrix.") filtered_expr_data <- expr_data } else { cat("Variance threshold (", variance_quantile * 100, "th percentile): ", variance_threshold, "\n", sep = "") } } } else { cat("Variance filtering disabled by user.\n") }
cat("Genes after variance filtering:", nrow(filtered_expr_data), "\n")
png("plots/01_gene_variance_distribution.png", width = 800, height = 600, res = plot_resolution) var_plot <- gene_variances[is.finite(gene_variances) & gene_variances > 0] if (length(var_plot) > 1) { hist(log10(var_plot), breaks = 50, main = "Log10 Variance Distribution of Genes", xlab = "Log10(Variance)", col = "lightblue", border = "black") if (is.finite(variance_threshold) && variance_threshold > 0) { abline(v = log10(variance_threshold), col = "red", lwd = 2, lty = 2) } } else { plot.new() text(0.5, 0.5, "Insufficient variance values for histogram") } dev.off()
datExpr <- t(filtered_expr_data)
if (nrow(datExpr) < 2) stop("Too few samples after filtering.") if (ncol(datExpr) < 2) stop("Too few genes after filtering.")
sampleTree <- hclust(dist(datExpr), method = "average") png("plots/02_sample_clustering.png", width = 1200, height = 600, res = plot_resolution) par(mar = c(2, 5, 2, 2)) plot(sampleTree, main = "Sample Clustering to Detect Outliers", sub = "Height represents dissimilarity between samples", xlab = "", cex = 0.7) abline(h = 200, col = "red", lwd = 2, lty = 2) dev.off()
png("plots/03_expression_data_distribution.png", width = 1200, height = 600, res = plot_resolution) par(mfrow = c(1, 2)) hist(as.numeric(datExpr), breaks = 100, main = "Distribution of Expression Values", sub = ifelse(log_transform, "Log2-transformed counts", "Input counts"), xlab = "Expression Value", col = "lightblue", border = "white") set.seed(123) sel_genes <- sample(seq_len(ncol(datExpr)), min(20, ncol(datExpr))) boxplot(datExpr[, sel_genes, drop = FALSE], main = "Gene Expression Distributions\n(20 random genes)", xlab = "Genes", ylab = "Expression Value", las = 2, cex.axis = 0.7, col = "lightblue", border = "gray40") par(mfrow = c(1, 1)) dev.off()
## ── STEP 3: Prepare trait data ─────────────────────────────────────────────── if (ncol(meta) < 2) { stop("Metadata must have sample names in the first column and at least one trait column.") }
sample_column <- colnames(meta)[1] if (anyDuplicated(meta[[sample_column]]) > 0) { stop("Duplicate sample names found in first column of metadata.") }
## ─── resolve trait column tokens (names or 1-based indices) ─── trait_indices <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(trait_tokens)) if (all(!is.na(trait_indices))) { if (any(trait_indices < 1 | trait_indices > ncol(meta))) { bad <- trait_indices[trait_indices < 1 | trait_indices > ncol(meta)] stop("Trait column index out of range: ", paste(bad, collapse = ", ")) } trait_columns <- colnames(meta)[trait_indices] } else { missing <- setdiff(trait_tokens, colnames(meta)) if (length(missing) > 0) { stop("Trait column(s) not found: ", paste(missing, collapse = ", ")) } trait_columns <- trait_tokens }
meta_subset <- meta[, c(sample_column, trait_columns), drop = FALSE] trait_data <- prepare_trait_matrix(meta_subset, sample_col = 1)
sample_ids <- rownames(datExpr) missing_samples <- setdiff(sample_ids, rownames(trait_data)) if (length(missing_samples) > 0) { stop("Trait data missing sample(s): ", paste(missing_samples, collapse = ", ")) } trait_data <- trait_data[sample_ids, , drop = FALSE]
for (col in colnames(trait_data)) { trait_data[[col]] <- as.numeric(trait_data[[col]]) }
cat("Trait data prepared:", ncol(trait_data), "trait(s)\n") print(head(trait_data))
## ── STEP 4: Network construction ───────────────────────────────────────────── if (run_sample_gene_qc) { gsg <- goodSamplesGenes(datExpr, verbose = 3) if (!gsg[["allOK"]]) { datExpr <- datExpr[gsg[["goodSamples"]], gsg[["goodGenes"]], drop = FALSE] trait_data <- trait_data[rownames(datExpr), , drop = FALSE] } } else { cat("WGCNA goodSamplesGenes filtering disabled by user.\n") }
cat("After goodSamplesGenes:", ncol(datExpr), "genes,", nrow(datExpr), "samples\n") if (ncol(datExpr) < min_module_size) { stop("Too few genes (", ncol(datExpr), ") for min_module_size=", min_module_size) }
set.seed(12345) net <- blockwiseModules( datExpr, power = soft_power, networkType = network_type, TOMType = tom_type, corType = cor_type, minModuleSize = min_module_size, reassignThreshold = 0, mergeCutHeight = merge_cut_height, numericLabels = TRUE, pamRespectsDendro = FALSE, saveTOMs = TRUE, saveTOMFileBase = "WGCNA_TOM", nThreads = n_threads, verbose = 3 )
module_colors <- labels2colors(net[["colors"]]) names(module_colors) <- colnames(datExpr)
module_gene_counts <- table(module_colors) ## grey is the placeholder in WGCNA for unassigned genes so we exclude it when counting biologically meaningful modules ## any co-expression module can be assigned to grey, so it is excluded from the reported module count. cat("Modules identified (excluding grey):", sum(names(module_gene_counts) != "grey"), "\n") print(module_gene_counts)
png("plots/05_dendrogram_and_modules.png", width = 1200, height = 600, res = plot_resolution) plotDendroAndColors( net[["dendrograms"]][[1]], module_colors[net[["blockGenes"]][[1]]], "Module Colors", dendroLabels = FALSE, hang = 0.03, addGuide = TRUE, guideHang = 0.05, main = "Gene Clustering Dendrogram and Module Colors" ) dev.off()
## ── STEP 5: Module eigengenes ──────────────────────────────────────────────── module_eigengenes <- orderMEs(net[["MEs"]])
if (ncol(module_eigengenes) > 1) { ME_cor <- cor(module_eigengenes, use = "pairwise.complete.obs") ME_diss <- 1 - ME_cor ME_tree <- hclust(as.dist(ME_diss), method = "average") png("plots/06_module_eigengene_dendrogram.png", width = 1000, height = 700, res = plot_resolution) plot(ME_tree, main = "Clustering of Module Eigengenes", xlab = "", sub = "", cex = 0.9) abline(h = 0.25, col = "red", lty = 2) dev.off() } else { cat("Only one module found; skipping module eigengene dendrogram.\n") png("plots/06_module_eigengene_dendrogram.png", width = 1000, height = 700, res = plot_resolution) plot.new() text(0.5, 0.5, "Only one module found;\ndendrogram not applicable", cex = 1.2) dev.off() }
## ── STEP 6: Module-trait associations ──────────────────────────────────────── trait_data_aligned <- trait_data[rownames(module_eigengenes), , drop = FALSE] module_trait_cor <- cor(module_eigengenes, trait_data_aligned, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
if (is.null(rownames(module_trait_cor))) rownames(module_trait_cor) <- colnames(module_eigengenes) if (is.null(colnames(module_trait_cor))) colnames(module_trait_cor) <- colnames(trait_data_aligned)
nSamples <- nrow(datExpr) module_trait_pval <- corPvalueStudent(module_trait_cor, nSamples)
text_matrix <- paste0(signif(module_trait_cor, 2), "\n(", signif(module_trait_pval, 1), ")") dim(text_matrix) <- dim(module_trait_cor)
## Scale the heatmap canvas with the number of traits/modules being plotted ## so that labels stay readable even with many traits or many modules; the ## floor values below match the previous fixed 1200x800 default for a ## typical small run. heatmap_width <- max(1200, 100 * ncol(trait_data_aligned) + 600) heatmap_height <- max(800, 40 * ncol(module_eigengenes) + 500)
png("plots/07_module_trait_heatmap.png", width = heatmap_width, height = heatmap_height, res = plot_resolution) par(mar = c(8, 10, 3, 3)) labeledHeatmap( Matrix = module_trait_cor, xLabels = colnames(trait_data_aligned), yLabels = colnames(module_eigengenes), ySymbols = colnames(module_eigengenes), colorLabels = FALSE, colors = blueWhiteRed(50), textMatrix = text_matrix, setStdMargins = FALSE, cex.text = 0.6, zlim = c(-1, 1), main = "Module-Trait Relationships\n(correlation and p-value)" ) dev.off()
## ── STEP 7 & 8: GS/MM and hub genes for each trait ───────────────────────── ## Gene significance, module membership, and hub-gene analysis are run ## independently for every trait column (numeric or one-hot encoded categorical). safe_label <- function(x) gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9._-]", "_", x)
common_samples <- Reduce(intersect, list( rownames(datExpr), rownames(trait_data_aligned), rownames(module_eigengenes) )) datExpr <- datExpr[common_samples, , drop = FALSE] trait_data_aligned <- trait_data_aligned[common_samples, , drop = FALSE] module_eigengenes <- module_eigengenes[common_samples, , drop = FALSE] nSamples <- nrow(datExpr)
module_trait_cor_sub <- cor(module_eigengenes, trait_data_aligned, use = "pairwise.complete.obs") if (is.null(rownames(module_trait_cor_sub))) rownames(module_trait_cor_sub) <- colnames(module_eigengenes) if (is.null(colnames(module_trait_cor_sub))) colnames(module_trait_cor_sub) <- colnames(trait_data_aligned)
for (trait_of_interest in colnames(trait_data_aligned)) { cat("\nAnalyzing trait:", trait_of_interest, "\n")
gene_significance <- cor(datExpr, trait_data_aligned[[trait_of_interest]], use = "pairwise.complete.obs") gene_significance_pval <- corPvalueStudent(as.numeric(gene_significance), nSamples) gene_significance <- as.numeric(gene_significance) names(gene_significance) <- colnames(datExpr) names(gene_significance_pval) <- colnames(datExpr)
## The most relevant module is the one whose eigengene shows the strongest ## absolute correlation with this trait. trait_vec <- abs(module_trait_cor_sub[, trait_of_interest, drop = TRUE]) best_idx <- which.max(trait_vec) most_relevant_module <- rownames(module_trait_cor_sub)[best_idx] module_id_clean <- sub("^ME", "", most_relevant_module)
if (!is.na(suppressWarnings(as.numeric(module_id_clean)))) { module_name <- labels2colors(as.numeric(module_id_clean)) } else { module_name <- module_id_clean }
cat("Most relevant module:", most_relevant_module, "->", module_name, "\n")
module_genes <- names(module_colors)[module_colors == module_name] cat("Genes in module", module_name, ":", length(module_genes), "\n") if (length(module_genes) == 0) { warning("No genes found in module ", module_name, " for trait ", trait_of_interest, "; skipping.") next }
gene_module_membership <- cor( datExpr[, module_genes, drop = FALSE], module_eigengenes[, most_relevant_module, drop = TRUE], use = "pairwise.complete.obs" )
module_gene_info <- data.frame( Gene = module_genes, ModuleMembership = as.numeric(gene_module_membership), GeneSignificance = as.numeric(gene_significance[module_genes]), MM_pvalue = corPvalueStudent(as.numeric(gene_module_membership), nSamples), GS_pvalue = as.numeric(gene_significance_pval[module_genes]), stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) module_gene_info <- module_gene_info[order(-abs(module_gene_info[["ModuleMembership"]])), ]
trait_label <- safe_label(trait_of_interest)
png(paste0("plots/08_gene_significance_vs_module_membership_", module_name, "_", trait_label, ".png"), width = 1000, height = 700, res = plot_resolution) plot(abs(module_gene_info[["ModuleMembership"]]), abs(module_gene_info[["GeneSignificance"]]), xlab = paste("Module Membership in", module_name, "module"), ylab = paste("Gene Significance for", trait_of_interest), main = paste("MM vs GS in", module_name, "module"), pch = 20, col = module_name, cex = 1) abline(lm(abs(GeneSignificance) ~ abs(ModuleMembership), data = module_gene_info), col = "red", lwd = 2) dev.off()
write.table(module_gene_info, file = paste0("results/module_gene_info_", module_name, "_", trait_label, ".tsv"), sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, row.names = FALSE)
adjacency_matrix <- adjacency(datExpr[, module_genes, drop = FALSE], power = soft_power, type = network_type, corFnc = cor_fnc) connectivity <- colSums(adjacency_matrix) - 1
hub_info <- data.frame( Gene = module_genes, ModuleMembership = as.numeric(gene_module_membership), GeneSignificance = as.numeric(gene_significance[module_genes]), Connectivity = as.numeric(connectivity), stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) hub_info <- hub_info[order(-hub_info[["Connectivity"]]), ]
write.table(hub_info, file = paste0("results/hub_genes_", module_name, "_", trait_label, ".tsv"), sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, row.names = FALSE)
is_top10 <- hub_info[["Connectivity"]] > quantile(hub_info[["Connectivity"]], 0.9, na.rm = TRUE)
png(paste0("plots/09_hub_genes_", module_name, "_", trait_label, ".png"), width = 800, height = 600, res = plot_resolution) plot(hub_info[["ModuleMembership"]], hub_info[["Connectivity"]], xlab = "Module Membership (MM)", ylab = "Connectivity (Intramodular)", main = paste("Hub Gene Identification in", module_name, "module"), pch = 20, col = ifelse(is_top10, "red", "black")) legend("topright", legend = c("Top 10% connected", "Other genes"), col = c("red", "black"), pch = 20, bty = "n") dev.off() }
## ── STEP 9: Co-expression network visualization ─────────────────────────────── gene_var <- apply(datExpr, 2, var, na.rm = TRUE) n_top <- min(1000, length(gene_var)) top_genes <- names(sort(gene_var, decreasing = TRUE))[1:n_top] cat("Calculating TOM for top", n_top, "variable genes...\n")
adj_subset <- adjacency(datExpr[, top_genes, drop = FALSE], power = soft_power, type = network_type, corFnc = cor_fnc) tom_subset <- TOMsimilarity(adj_subset, TOMType = tom_type) colnames(tom_subset) <- top_genes rownames(tom_subset) <- top_genes
tom_thresh <- quantile(tom_subset[upper.tri(tom_subset)], 0.95, na.rm = TRUE) adj_thresh <- tom_subset adj_thresh[adj_thresh < tom_thresh] <- 0 diag(adj_thresh) <- 0
network_graph <- graph_from_adjacency_matrix(adj_thresh, mode = "undirected", weighted = TRUE, diag = FALSE) gene_color_map <- setNames(module_colors, colnames(datExpr)) node_colors <- gene_color_map[top_genes] node_colors[is.na(node_colors)] <- "grey" node_sizes <- log10(degree(network_graph) + 1) * 3 + 3
png("plots/14_coexpression_network.png", width = 1200, height = 1200, res = plot_resolution) par(mar = c(1, 1, 3, 1)) set.seed(123) plot(network_graph, vertex.size = node_sizes, vertex.label = NA, vertex.color = node_colors, vertex.frame.color = NA, edge.width = 0.4, edge.color = "gray80", layout = layout_with_fr(network_graph), main = paste0("Co-expression Network\n(Top ", n_top, " Variable Genes, Top 5% Connections)")) unique_colors <- unique(node_colors[node_colors != "grey"]) if (length(unique_colors) <= 10) { legend("topright", legend = unique_colors, col = unique_colors, pch = 19, cex = 0.7, title = "Modules", bty = "n") } dev.off()
## ── STEP 10: Module eigengene network ──────────────────────────────────────── me_correlation <- cor(module_eigengenes, use = "pairwise.complete.obs") me_pvalue <- corPvalueStudent(me_correlation, nSamples)
cor_thresh <- me_cor_threshold me_adj <- (abs(me_correlation) > cor_thresh) & (me_pvalue < 0.05) diag(me_adj) <- FALSE n_edges <- sum(me_adj) / 2 cat("\nNumber of module pairs with |correlation| >", cor_thresh, "and p < 0.05:", n_edges, "\n")
if (n_edges == 0) { fallback_thresh <- me_cor_threshold / 2 cat("\nNo edges at |r| >", cor_thresh, "; trying |r| >", fallback_thresh, "...\n") cor_thresh <- fallback_thresh me_adj <- (abs(me_correlation) > cor_thresh) & (me_pvalue < 0.05) diag(me_adj) <- FALSE n_edges <- sum(me_adj) / 2 cat("Edges at |r| >", cor_thresh, ":", n_edges, "\n") } me_names <- colnames(module_eigengenes) module_ids_me <- sub("^ME", "", me_names) module_colors_me <- module_ids_me numeric_me <- suppressWarnings(!is.na(as.numeric(module_ids_me))) module_colors_me[numeric_me] <- labels2colors(as.numeric(module_ids_me[numeric_me])) names(module_colors_me) <- me_names
if (sum(me_adj) > 0) { me_network <- graph_from_adjacency_matrix(me_adj, mode = "undirected", diag = FALSE) vertex_colors <- module_colors_me[vertex_attr(me_network, "name")] vertex_colors[is.na(vertex_colors)] <- "grey" vertex_attr(me_network, "color") <- vertex_colors
module_sizes_table <- table(module_colors) module_sizes_net <- module_sizes_table[vertex_colors] module_sizes_net[is.na(module_sizes_net)] <- 10 node_sizes_me <- log10(as.numeric(module_sizes_net) + 1) * 10 + 10
png("plots/15_module_eigengene_network.png", width = 1000, height = 1000, res = plot_resolution) par(mar = c(1, 1, 3, 1)) set.seed(123) plot(me_network, vertex.size = node_sizes_me, vertex.label = vertex_colors, vertex.label.cex = 0.7, vertex.label.color = "black", vertex.color = vertex_colors, vertex.frame.color = "black", edge.width = 2.5, edge.color = "gray40", layout = layout_with_fr(me_network), main = paste0("Module Eigengene Network\n(|correlation| > ", cor_thresh, ", p < 0.05)")) dev.off() } else { ## Always emit this plot (even as a placeholder) so downstream output ## discovery always finds the same, deterministic set of plot files. cat("No significant module-module edges after thresholding.\n") png("plots/15_module_eigengene_network.png", width = 1000, height = 1000, res = plot_resolution) par(mar = c(1, 1, 3, 1)) plot.new() text(0.5, 0.5, "No significant module-module\nedges found", cex = 1.2) dev.off() }
me_cor_labeled <- me_correlation rownames(me_cor_labeled) <- module_colors_me colnames(me_cor_labeled) <- module_colors_me
png("plots/16_module_eigengene_correlation_heatmap.png", width = 1200, height = 1200, res = plot_resolution) if (nrow(me_cor_labeled) > 1) { heatmap(me_cor_labeled, col = colorRampPalette(c("blue", "white", "red"))(50), scale = "none", symm = TRUE, margins = c(8, 8), main = "Module Eigengene Correlation Heatmap") } else { par(mar = c(5, 5, 4, 2)) image(x = 1, y = 1, z = matrix(me_cor_labeled[1, 1], nrow = 1), col = colorRampPalette(c("blue", "white", "red"))(50), zlim = c(-1, 1), axes = FALSE, main = "Module Eigengene Correlation Heatmap") axis(1, at = 1, labels = colnames(me_cor_labeled), las = 2) axis(2, at = 1, labels = rownames(me_cor_labeled), las = 2) } dev.off()
cat("\nWGCNA pipeline complete.\n") cat("Plots written to: plots/\n") cat("Results written to: results/\n")

param.name: counts_file
param.label: Counts matrix
param.help: Genes as rows, samples as columns. First column must be gene IDs.

param.name: metadata_file
param.label: Sample metadata
param.help: First column should be sample name. Trait columns can be numeric or categorical.

param.name: softpower_config
param.label: Soft-thresholding config
param.help: Config file output from the 'WGCNA: Soft-Thresholding Power' tool. If provided, soft power is read automatically and the manual value below is ignored.

param.name: soft_power
param.label: Soft-thresholding power (used only if no config file is provided)
param.help: Ignored when a soft-thresholding config file is supplied above.

param.name: tom_type
param.label: TOM type
param.help: Should match the network type. Signed TOM is recommended when using a signed network.

param.name: cor_type
param.label: Correlation type
param.help: Pearson correlation is the standard choice. Biweight midcorrelation (bicor) is a robust alternative that is less sensitive to outlier samples.

param.name: min_module_size
param.label: Minimum module size
param.help: Minimum number of genes required to form a module.

param.name: merge_cut_height
param.label: Module merge cut height
param.help: Modules with eigengene correlation above (1 - this value) will be merged.

param.name: me_cor_threshold
param.label: Module eigengene network correlation threshold
param.help: Minimum |correlation| between module eigengenes required to draw an edge in the module eigengene network (Step 10). If no module pairs pass this threshold, the tool automatically retries at half this value.

param.name: run_sample_gene_qc
param.label: Run WGCNA sample/gene QC filtering
param.help: Use goodSamplesGenes to remove samples or genes that fail WGCNA checks.

param.name: trait_columns
param.label: Metadata trait columns
param.help: Select one or more trait columns from the metadata file. Numeric columns are used as-is; categorical columns are one-hot encoded. Gene significance, module membership, and hub-gene analysis are run for every selected trait (and each one-hot level of categorical traits).

param.name: plot_resolution
param.label: Plot resolution (DPI)
param.help: Resolution, in dots per inch, used when rendering the PNG plots.

param.name: save_rscript
param.label: Save the R script used for this run
param.help: Useful for debugging. If enabled, the exact R script executed by this tool is provided as an additional output.

collection.name: gene_tables
collection.label: ${tool.name} on ${on_string} - Module and hub gene tables

has_text.text: Sample names in the counts matrix columns do not match the sample names in the first column of the metadata file.

tool.help:
**WGCNA Full Pipeline**
Runs the complete Weighted Gene Co-expression Network Analysis (WGCNA) workflow:
1. Data loading and QC (optional log2 transform and variance filtering) 2. Module detection with blockwiseModules 3. Module eigengene analysis 4. Module-trait correlation analysis 5. Gene significance/module membership analysis 6. Hub-gene ranking 7. Co-expression and eigengene network visualization
**Inputs**
- **Counts matrix** — TSV with genes as rows and samples as columns. First column must be gene IDs. - **Sample metadata** — TSV with first column as sample names matching count matrix columns. Trait columns can be numeric or categorical (categorical columns are one-hot encoded automatically). Gene significance, module membership, and hub-gene outputs are produced for every selected trait. - **Soft-thresholding config** — Config file output from the *WGCNA: Soft-Thresholding Power* tool. If provided, soft power is read automatically from this file.
**Network type, TOM type, and correlation type**
Network type defaults to *Unsigned*. For most RNA-seq co-expression analyses, *Signed* (or *Signed hybrid*) is recommended instead, since it preserves the distinction between positive and negative correlations — set Network type and TOM type to matching signed/unsigned values. Correlation type defaults to Pearson; choose biweight midcorrelation (bicor) for a robust alternative that down-weights outlier samples.
**Outputs**
- **wgcna_plots** — a collection of every diagnostic and results plot produced by the pipeline. - **gene_tables** — a collection of module gene info and hub gene tables, one pair per trait (and per one-hot level for categorical traits), each for the module most correlated with that trait. - **R script** (optional) — the exact R script executed for this run, provided when "Save the R script used for this run" is enabled; useful for debugging.
